pages.raw_text:
An examination Is being held by
the Northern Pacific today at which
the eyesight and hearing Of the en-
ployes are being tested.


A. P. Bryan. superintendent of the
o. r. & N, is in the city.


Officials of the Q. r. & N. here
have received notification that the
company will take care of and edu-
cate the three children Of J. M. Ker-
nan, one of their agents at Heppner,
who persisted In the recent floods. He
remained at his telegraph instrument
to warn the people down the valley
and was caught at his post.


Superintendent A. Beamer OF the
Northern Pacific has posted a new
list of rules for the disregarding of
which the employees will be discipiin-
ed by being laid of from five to
80 days and by dismissal from the
service Of the company.


John s. Mercer local agent OF the
Wisconsin Central system, leaves for
North Yakima tonight.


III. s. Collins OF the Northwestern
line leaves for Kalispsll, Mont, to-
night.


J. F. Hannaford. second vice presi-
dent and general traffic manager Of
the Northern Pacific. left last night
for Tacoma.


Several changes have been made in
the freight department Of the Great
Northern. Cashier Dutton has been
appointed to the position Of chief
clerk and a new man named Thomp-
son has been made cashier.
